task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenToonz application is open, and the title bar or project browser clearly displays 'dwanko_run.tnz'.

task_completion.description: Set an initial keyframe for Column 1 at Frame 1.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Xsheet or Timeline panel, Frame 1 of Column 1 shows a keyframe indicator (typically a key icon or specific cell highlighting).

task_completion.description: Set a second keyframe for Column 1 at Frame 20, moving the content to the right.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Frame 20 of Column 1 shows a keyframe indicator in the Xsheet/Timeline, and the character/content in the main viewer is positioned further to the right compared to its position at Frame 1.

task_completion.description: Apply linear post-extrapolation to the animation curve so the movement continues indefinitely.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Function Editor or animation curve window is visible, showing the position curve extending linearly past Frame 20, OR an extrapolation setting/dropdown is visibly set to 'Linear' for the relevant parameter.

task_completion.description: Render Frame 20 to the specified output directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Output Settings dialog shows the save path as '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/drift/' and the frame range includes Frame 20, or a terminal/file manager shows 'frame0020.png' successfully created in that directory.

task_completion.description: Render Frame 60 to the specified output directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Output Settings dialog shows the frame range including Frame 60, or a terminal/file manager shows 'frame0060.png' successfully created in the '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/drift/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenToonz GUI to perform the animation tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show active interaction with the OpenToonz interface (e.g., clicking menus, adjusting the timeline, using the Function Editor) rather than editing the .tnz file directly in a text editor.

integrity.description: The agent used extrapolation rather than manually keyframing Frame 60.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Xsheet or Timeline does NOT show a manually placed keyframe at Frame 60 for Column 1. The movement at Frame 60 should be driven entirely by the extrapolation setting.

integrity.description: The rendered images were generated by OpenToonz.
integrity.visual_evidence: The OpenToonz render progress bar, Output Settings, or Fast Render dialog is visible, matching the creation of the final output files.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The scene file is located at '/home/ga/OpenToonz/samples/dwanko_run.tnz'. The output directory is '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/drift/'. The rendered frames must be named 'frame0020.png' and 'frame0060.png'. The animation must move the character to the right, meaning the x-coordinate of the character's centroid in Frame 60 must be strictly greater than in Frame 20. There must be a minimum absolute pixel difference ratio of 0.01 between Frame 20 and Frame 60.
